lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: Well met! Are you interested in hearing about the art of smithing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I train my smithing?

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: To be able to smith anything, you're going to need one of these beauties.

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: I see you already have a hammer. Nice one.

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: You're going to need to get your hand on some metal bars. You could do this by mining your own ores and smelting them at a furnace.

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: There is a furnace located in Lumbridge, just north of the castle opposite the general store.

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: If you are looking for some ore, there is a mine east of Varrock. There you can find some copper and tin ore. Don't forget to bring a pickaxe.

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: When you have your bars, bring them to an anvil to open the smithing interface. If the item name is in black, this means you do not have the level to smith the item.

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: If the item name is in white, this means you do have the level to smith the item. You will see the bars required to smith the item underneath the name of the item.

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: If the number of bars is in orange, this means you do not have enough bars to smith the item. If it is in green, this means you have enough bars for the item.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the advice.

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: Is there anything else you would like to know?

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: You might find it beneficial to keep a nice stack of bars in your bank before you start smithing. Unless you like to jump from task to task of course.

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: If you want to smith something that you may not have the level for, you could try gaining some temporary knowledge in smithing from a boost.

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: You can buy a Dwarven stout at the Rising Sun Inn in Falador.

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: If you are looking for a quest, you should talk to the squire in the courtyard of the white knight's castle. He is looking for a bit of help with something. I would recommend you be a little experienced with combat

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: though.

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: Is there anything else you would like to know?

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: Cor blimey, I don't think there is anything I can teach you, but I'll do my best.

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: As you get better you'll find you will be able to smith Mithril and eventually Adamantite and even Runite. This can be very lucrative but very expensive on the coal front.

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: It may be worth you stockpiling coal for a while before attempting these difficult metals or even sticking to good old reliable iron by the bucket load.

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: If you want to stop yourself from failing to smith iron, I suggest purchasing some rings of forging. Be aware that they will break after a certain number of bars are smelted.

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: If you want to save on coal, you could try smithing your bars over at the Blast Furnace in Keldagrim.

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: If you are looking for something more interesting to smith, you could talk to Otto Godblessed about smithing hastae.

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: Though he may ask you to complete some tasks for him. You can find him south of the barbarian outpost and west of the whirlpool.

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: Is there anything else you would like to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What kind of things can I smith?

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: There are many things you can make, from weapons to your good old fashioned armour.

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: Weapons are the cheapest to smith. They range from a measly one bar, all the way to three bars.

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: Armour can be the costliest item to smith, the cost of each item ranges from a measly one bar all the way up to a whopping five bars.

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: Some weapons and armour, such as darts, will require you to have gained knowledge on how to smith them. This is due to the complex nature of the weapon.

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: You might find that other items don't require conventional bars you would gather. Some may require you to piece blades back together or even infuse a crystal into a piece of armour.

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: Is there anything else you want to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not right now, thank you.

lines.speaker: Master smithing tutor
lines.text: Well, just come back any time you want to know anything!
